title: OCR Error Detection Hotfix
body:
Fix input handling of error detection model: 
- Segment length is automatically handled by tokenizer, and is by `#tokens`, not `#chars`, removing the splitting logic inside `marker`
- Join lines using `\n`

step_plan.summary: Modify PdfProvider.check_line_spans to assemble page text by concatenating spans per logical line and joining those lines with '\n', removing ad-hoc span-level leading-space + per-span-newline concatenation so downstream OCR error-detection receives properly line-joined input.

steps.operation: Change page text assembly to join logical lines with '\n' and stop span-level leading-space + per-span-newline concatenation
steps.reason: The function currently flattens spans and builds a text blob by prepending a space and appending a newline per span (e.g. text += ' ' + span_text + '\n'), which produces incorrect spacing/newline semantics and attempts a character/span-level segmentation. Tokenizers (downstream) handle segmentation by tokens. Build each logical line by concatenating its spans (as-is) into a single line string, then join those line strings with '\n' to produce the page text passed to OCR error-detection models.
steps.side_effects: Behavioral: Downstream consumers will now see a text blob where logical lines are separated by single '\n' characters instead of many span-level newlines/spaces. This may change whitespace and newline counts compared to previous behavior—verify callers that expected per-span newlines or leading spaces. Tests: Run PDF/ocr-related tests (e.g., tests/builders/test_ocr_pipeline.py) and full test suite to catch formatting-dependent assertions. API/Integration: OCR/error-detection models and any validators that assumed the old span-level formatting should continue to work (and will likely be more correct), but verify integrations that parsed newlines or counted characters. Backwards compatibility: If other code relied on the previous leading-space-per-span pattern, that code may need adjustment. Also check for double-newline scenarios if OCR line spans are already stored with trailing newlines elsewhere (e.g., builders/ocr.py may append '\n' to spans) and adjust accordingly after test runs.
